title: ADR Frontmatter Schema
description: Frontmatter schema for Architecture Decision Records authored under docs/planning/adrs/NNNN-{kebab-case}.md. Aligns with MADR v4.0.0 plus the hve-core ADR Planner extensions for ID, lineage, and Architecturally Significant Requirement (ASR) triggers.

id.description: Four-digit zero-padded ADR identifier scoped to the owning project_slug (e.g. '0001').

description.description: One-line summary of the decision used by the docs frontmatter validator and search indexes.

author.description: Optional primary author or owning role for docs metadata.

ms.date.description: Optional YYYY-MM-DD publish date for docs metadata.

status.description: Canonical six-value ADR status taxonomy from adr-standards.instructions.md (MADR v4.0.0 plus 'withdrawn' for proposals retired before acceptance or rejection).

proposed_date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the ADR was first proposed. Required for every ADR regardless of status.

accepted_date.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) the decision was accepted. Required when status='accepted' (enforced by validator). Null until acceptance.

effort.description: T-shirt sizing of implementation effort. S=days, M=weeks, L=quarter, XL=multi-quarter.

affected_components.description: Repository-relative paths, package names, or named components materially changed by this decision. At least one entry required; use ['none'] only for purely informational decisions.

metric.description: Named, observable metric (e.g., 'p95 cold-start latency', 'frontmatter-validation pass rate').

target.description: Concrete target value or threshold (e.g., '<200ms', '>=99%', 'zero CI failures over 30 days').

measurement_window.description: Window over which the metric is evaluated (e.g., '30 days post-merge', 'per-PR', 'first production week').

source.description: Where the metric is read from (e.g., 'logs/pester-summary.json', 'Application Insights', 'GitHub Actions run summary').

success_criteria.description: Measurable, time-bound criteria for declaring this decision successful. Required (non-empty) when asr_triggers is non-empty (enforced by validator).

deciders.description: People or roles accountable for the decision.

consulted.description: Subject-matter experts consulted with two-way communication.

informed.description: Stakeholders kept up-to-date with one-way communication.

supersedes.description: Single-parent supersession (GP-06): the four-digit ID of the prior ADR this decision supersedes within the same project.

superseded-by.description: Four-digit ID of the ADR that supersedes this one, when applicable.

path.description: Repository-relative path to the related ADR or artifact.

related.description: Cross-references to other ADRs or supporting artifacts.

evidence.description: Required citation, link, or quoted constraint that justifies the trigger.

asr_triggers.description: Architecturally Significant Requirements that motivated the decision.

migrated-from.description: Provenance: relative path to the source template or prior format the ADR was migrated from.

migrated-on.description: Provenance: ISO 8601 date the migration completed.

migrated-by.description: Provenance: tool or person responsible for the migration.

original-id.description: Provenance: identifier used in the prior system before migration.

driverToTriggerMap.description: Map from Decision Driver labels (free-text strings matching the driver bullets in the body) to ASR trigger references; populated by the ADR Planner at Decide exit and enforced by Validate-AdrConsistency rule ADR-CONSISTENCY-008 (driver-trigger-map-complete). Map keys are substring-matched against driver bullet text after normalization, so they may be short labels or full sentences as long as each driver has at least one corresponding key.

decisionMetadata.description: Optional planner-internal metadata serialized into ADR frontmatter. Mirrors `state.json.decisionMetadata` from the ADR Planner; not authored by hand.
